title: Major Burns - Fluid Resuscitation
chief_complaint: House fire. Trapped in burning room for 5 minutes. Burns to face, neck, anterior trunk, both arms. Singed nasal hairs. Hoarse voice. Carbonaceous sputum.

physical_exam.Airway: Singed nasal hairs, eyebrows. Carbonaceous sputum. Hoarse voice. Stridor present (inhalation injury - EARLY intubation indicated).
physical_exam.Burns: Face, neck, anterior trunk, bilateral upper extremities (circumferential). Mixed partial and full thickness. Total BSA 45% (Rule of 9s: Head 9% + Anterior trunk 18% + Arms 9% each = 45%). Leathery, waxy white eschar on chest (full thickness). No sensation in full thickness areas. Painful in partial thickness areas.
physical_exam.Extremities: Circumferential burns both arms. Pulses dopplerable. Capillary refill 3 seconds. Tense compartments (escharotomy may be needed).
physical_exam.Lungs: Stridor. Bilateral wheezing (bronchospasm from inhalation injury).

hidden_data.abg: pH 7.28, PaCO2 55, PaO2 58, COHb 25% (severe carbon monoxide poisoning - normal <3%, toxic >20%)
hidden_data.bronchoscopy: Airway edema, mucosal sloughing, carbonaceous debris throughout tracheobronchial tree (severe inhalation injury).

correct_diagnosis: Major Burns (45% TBSA - Mixed Partial and Full Thickness) with Inhalation Injury, Carbon Monoxide Poisoning, and Circumferential Extremity Burns
key_learning_points: Burn depth: Superficial (1st - sunburn, epidermis only, painful, no blisters). Partial thickness (2nd - dermis involved, blisters, painful). Superficial partial (heals in 7-14 days). Deep partial (heals in 3-8 weeks, may need grafting). Full thickness (3rd - subcutaneous tissue, leathery, painless, requires grafting). 4th degree (muscle, bone)

TBSA estimation: Rule of 9s (adults): Head 9%, Each arm 9%, Anterior trunk 18%, Posterior trunk 18%, Each leg 18%, Perineum 1%. Palmar surface (patient's palm) = 1%. Lund-Browder charts for children (different head:body ratio)

Fluid resuscitation (Parkland Formula): 4mL × kg × %TBSA = total LR in first 24h. Give HALF in first 8h from time of injury (not time of arrival!), HALF over remaining 16h. Example: 4 × 70kg × 45% = 12,600mL. First 8h: 6,300mL at 788mL/hr. Titrate to UOP 0.5mL/kg/hr (30-50mL/hr). Adjust rate hourly based on UOP

Airway and inhalation injury: EARLY intubation for: Stridor, hoarseness, singed nasal hairs, carbonaceous sputum, facial burns, bronchoscopic findings. Inhalation injury = leading cause of death in burns. Carbon monoxide poisoning: 100% O2, hyperbaric O2 if COHb >25% or neurologic symptoms

Burn wound care: Clean with chlorhexidine. Apply Silvadene (silver sulfadiazine) or Bacitracin. Non-adherent gauze. Circumferential extremity burns: Monitor for compartment syndrome (serial Doppler, compartment pressures). Escharotomy if: Pulselessness, Compartment pressures >30, Neurologic deficit. Fasciotomy if escharotomy insufficient. Early excision and grafting (within 48-72h) for deep partial and full thickness burns reduces mortality and length of stay

Nutrition: Hypermetabolic state (2× BMR). Early enteral nutrition (within 6-12h). High protein, high calorie. Vitamin C, zinc, copper supplementation. Metabolic cart to guide caloric needs. Immunizations: Tetanus booster. Referral to burn center: >10% TBSA partial thickness, Any full thickness, Burns to face/hands/feet/genitalia/major joints, Inhalation injury, Electrical/chemical burns
